channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Não vamos deixar que potências usem minerais do Brasil, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that Brazil will not allow foreign powers to exploit its critical minerals again, accusing them of repeating historical patterns of extracting gold, silver, diamonds, enslaving Black people for 330 years, and committing violence against Indigenous peoples. He announced the creation of a special council to safeguard national sovereignty over critical minerals and rare earths, established in 2025 to advise on strategic decisions. The remarks were made at the opening of the 3rd CNDRSS, where the government signed 9 decrees for rural development, 7 for agrarian reform, and granted 17 land titles to quilombola territories.

item.title: TSE forma maioria pela inelegibilidade de Cláudio Castro
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) formed a majority to declare the inelegibility of former Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) and state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar (União Brasil) for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. The ruling is based on an alleged scheme of irregular hirings through Ceperj and UERJ, involving cash withdrawals totaling R$248 million, as per the Rio MP investigation. Castro resigned as governor one day before the ruling to avoid immediate loss of office and allow indirect succession via the state legislature.

item.title: Sem Ratinho Júnior, Flávio Bolsonaro pode absorver um terço de seus eleitores
item.description: The withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior (PSD) from the 2026 presidential race could reshape the electoral landscape, with Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) inheriting 32% of the Paraná governor’s voters, according to a Genial/Quaest poll. Lula (PT) and Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) follow with 17% and 13%, respectively, while 27% of Ratinho’s supporters opt for blank, null, or abstention votes. His absence suggests a reorganization of the right, with Bolsonaro capturing roughly a third of his orphaned electorate, potentially reducing fragmentation at the top of the race.

item.title: TSE forma maioria e condena Cláudio Castro à inelegibilidade
item.description: The TSE formed a majority to condemn former Rio governor Cláudio Castro (PL) for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, rendering him ineligible until 2030. The vote was 4-1, with support from ministers Isabel Gallotti, Antônio Carlos Ferreira, Floriano de Azevedo Marques, and Estela Aranha. Castro resigned the day before the ruling to avoid political fallout and now plans to run for Senate sub judice, hoping for a favorable STF decision to overturn his ineligibility.

item.title: Lula tenta reduzir de novo a fila do INSS
item.description: Lula is again attempting to reduce the backlog at the INSS, which reached 1.23 million pending requests when he took office in 2023. The government claims progress through digitalization and hiring, but delays persist due to high demand and procedural complexity. The effort is part of his administration's social security agenda.

item.title: TSE tem maioria para tornar ex-governador do Rio de Janeiro Cláudio Castro inelegível
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) formed a majority to declare former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro ineligible for eight years due to abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. Despite his resignation announced on Monday (22), the trial proceeded and resulted in favorable votes from Ministers Estela Aranha, Cármen Lúcia, Floriano de Azevedo Marques, and Antônio Carlos Ferreira. Minister Nunes Marques was the only one to vote against the conviction.

item.title: Nunes Marques vota para manter elegibilidade de Castro
item.description: Minister Kassio Nunes Marques of the Superior Electoral Court (TSE) voted to maintain the electoral eligibility of former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), in the trial investigating alleged abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. The vote was delivered on Tuesday, the 24th, concerning the use of the Ceperj Foundation and the State University of Rio de Janeiro's structure in the campaign.

item.title: Gleisi pede explicações de Campos Neto sobre caso Banco Master
item.description: Minister Gleisi Hoffmann demanded explanations from Roberto Campos Neto regarding the Banco Master case, accusing him of negligence despite warnings from the TCU, bank presidents, and the FGC about asset-backed credit operations posing systemic risk. She criticized his claim of no responsibility for monitoring the bank as 'shameless'. President Lula also holds Campos Neto accountable for authorizing, in October 2019, the transfer of control of Banco Máxima to Daniel Vorcaro, which led to the creation of Banco Master.

item.title: Nunes Marques vota para inocentar Cláudio Castro
item.description: Minister Nunes Marques of the TSE voted to acquit former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) in an eligibility case, citing insufficient evidence of political and economic abuse of power. Castro resigned one day before the judgment resumed and claimed to be leading Senate race polls. Rapporteur Isabel Gallotti had voted for conviction, alleging the use of state administrative structures for electoral gain.

item.title: Lula: Muita gente não gosta de mim porque são R$ 400 bi para o social; preferiam na especulação
item.description: President Lula stated that many people dislike him for allocating R$400 billion to social programs, claiming they would prefer the funds be used for monetary speculation instead. He did not name the critics but framed the opposition as a preference for financial gain over social investment.

item.title: Kassab reafirma apoio à reeleição de Tarcísio em SP
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, reaffirmed his support for Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' re-election in São Paulo, highlighting his performance and merit at the head of the state government. The endorsement was announced following the affiliation of Silvia Abravanel with the PSD and strengthens the alliance between the party and the governor, despite previous disagreements over a potential 2026 presidential candidacy for Tarcísio.

item.title: Nunes Marques vota para absolver Cláudio Castro; placar está em 2x1
item.description: Minister Kássio Nunes Marques voted to acquit former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), stating there was insufficient evidence and gravity to condemn him for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. The TSE vote stands at 2 to 1 in favor of conviction, with one minister's vote still pending to determine the final outcome.

item.title: TSE e TREs discutem ações de segurança para as Eleições de 2026
item.description: Under the leadership of President Minister Cármen Lúcia, the Superior Electoral Court (TSE) convened presidents of 25 out of 27 Regional Electoral Tribunals (TREs) and the Federal Police command to establish the strategic security plan for the 2026 elections. The meeting aimed to align protocols against electoral crimes, disinformation, and external interference, strengthening the role of police intelligence in monitoring the electoral process.

item.title: Moraes “teme que Bolsonaro venha a morrer”, diz Eduardo
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro claimed that the decision by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes to grant his father, Jair Bolsonaro, 90 days of humanitarian house arrest lacks legal foundation and is purely political. He argued that Moraes fears Bolsonaro may die in custody, and the measure is intended to avoid political accountability for such an outcome.

item.title: TSE tem 3 votos a 1 pela condenação de Cláudio Castro
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) recorded on Tuesday (24) a 3-1 vote in favor of declaring former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) ineligible due to abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. If upheld, the ruling would bar him from holding elected office for the next eight years.

item.title: Lula anuncia Fernanda Machiaveli como nova ministra do Desenvolvimento Agrário
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced Fernanda Machiaveli, current executive secretary of the Ministry of Agrarian Development (MDA), as the new minister of the portfolio. The appointment was made on Tuesday (24) in Brasília, reinforcing the government’s bet on continuity in agrarian policy focused on land reform and support for family farming. Machiaveli has already been part of the MDA’s management and is known for her technical work in rural development policies.

item.title: Nunes Marques vota para absolver Cláudio Castro em processo que pode deixá-lo inelegível
item.description: Minister Kassio Nunes Marques voted to acquit Cláudio Castro of abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, stating there is no proof of electoral motivation in the hiring of 27,6 thousand temporary workers. So far, the TSE verdict is tied 2-2, with Nunes Marques casting the first favorable vote for Castro. The final decision will occur in an extraordinary session called by Minister Cármen Lúcia this Wednesday.

item.title: Nunes Marques vota contra inelegibilidade de Cláudio Castro; placar está 2 a 1 pela condenação
item.description: Minister Nunes Marques of the TSE voted against the ineligibility of former governor Cláudio Castro (PL), keeping the vote at 2 to 1 in favor of conviction in the Superior Electoral Court. Despite Castro’s resignation announcement, the trial continues, with prior votes having favored cassation and ineligibility for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. The case involves irregularities at Ceperj and UERJ, including a secret payroll and electoral misuse of public funds.

item.title: Eduardo Leite rechaça disputa ao Senado e diz que só deixa governo do RS para concorrer ao Planalto
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) stated he will only leave office to run for President in 2026, ruling out a Senate bid. He positioned himself as a unifying figure capable of overcoming polarization, citing his unbroken electoral record and clean reputation. Leite said the challenges he faces in Rio Grande do Sul mirror those Brazil will encounter in 2027 and offered his name to serve the nation.

item.title: De olho no Planalto, Caiado deixa governo de Goiás em 31 de março
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) will step down on March 31, 2026, to seek the PSD's presidential nomination, competing with Eduardo Leite of Rio Grande do Sul. His departure will allow Vice Governor Daniel Vilela (MDB) to assume the state government, as the PSD aims to announce its presidential candidate by March 27. Caiado is seen as the favorite following Ratinho Junior's withdrawal, though Leite remains a strong alternative, technically tied in polls.

item.title: Ao vivo: TSE retoma julgamento de Cláudio Castro
item.description: The TSE resumes the trial of appeals seeking the annulment of the diploma and ineligibility of former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), following his resignation on March 23, 2026. The current score is 2-0 in favor of annulment, with votes from rapporteur Isabel Gallotti and minister Antonio Carlos Ferreira. The case examines alleged abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, including temporary hires via Ceperj and UERJ to benefit Castro’s re-election campaign.

item.title: Lula tem 47% de aprovação e 50% de desaprovação em ranking latino-americano
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) ranks 8th in Latin American presidential approval with 46.6% positive and 49.8% negative image, according to CB Consultora Opinión Pública’s March 2026 survey across 18 countries. His approval declined from February’s 49.2% positive and 47.5% negative, signaling a worsening public perception. The ranking is led by Mexico’s Claudia Sheinbaum (72.3%) and El Salvador’s Nayib Bukele (71.8%), while Venezuela’s Delcy Rodríguez (26.4%), Panama’s José Raúl Mulino (34.2%), and Ecuador’s Daniel Noboa (33.5%) rank lowest.

item.title: Desistência de Ratinho Jr embaralha eleição e favorece Flávio Bolsonaro, diz pesquisa
item.description: The withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior from the presidential pre-candidacy benefited Flávio Bolsonaro, who captured 32% of the former governor’s voter base, followed by Lula at 17% and Ronaldo Caiado at 13%. Meanwhile, 27% of Ratinho’s supporters would cast null, blank votes or abstain, indicating a vacuum in the moderate right. The Genial/Quaest March survey anticipated this scenario, and the April 15 poll will be the first to test the real race without the Paraná governor.

item.title: A articulação petista para afastar o presidente da CPMI do INSS
item.description: PT deputies sent a request to Congress President Davi Alcolumbre to remove Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG) as chair of the INSS CPMI, arguing he has lost the political and legal conditions to lead the commission due to alleged irregularities in Pix amendments to the Oasis Foundation and a vote-counting error regarding Lulinha’s bank secrecy lift. The request cites STF rulings, including a Flávio Dino order for Vianato explain the amendments and the prior annulment of a decision by Dino. The CPMI’s extension was approved by André Mendonça, with a final ruling by the plenary expected Thursday the 26th.

item.title: Lula diz ter pedido a Trump extradição de brasileiros investigados
item.description: Lula said he asked Trump to extradite Brazilians under investigation for crimes such as tax evasion, citing Ricardo Magro as an example, and offered cooperation from the Brazilian Federal Police and judiciary in fighting organized crime. He highlighted the creation of a PF base in Manaus to monitor borders and advocated prioritizing investigations into criminal leaders rather than peripheral operations. Lula also criticized U.S. actions in South America lacking proof of drug trafficking links and stated Brazil is ready to cooperate 100% in combating drug and arms trafficking.

item.title: Prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro deve diminuir pressão para derrubar veto do PL da Dosimetria
item.description: The house arrest of Jair Bolsonaro, ordered by Minister Alexandre de Moraes with an initial 90-day term, reduces pressure on Congress to overturn the veto of the Dosimetria Bill, which would benefit those convicted of attempting a coup, including the former president. Despite strict restrictions — electronic ankle monitor, ban on external communication and social media, and limited visits — the move eases tensions between the right-wing bloc and the STF, while increasing the likelihood of a CPI into the Banco Master case and impeachment proceedings against Supreme Court justices, viewed as more feasible in 2027.

item.title: “Lula está do lado dos criminosos”, diz Moro em evento do PL
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro formally joined the PL party during an event in Curitiba alongside Flávio Bolsonaro, the party's pre-candidate for president. At the event, Moro questioned the legitimacy of Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's election and accused the federal government of being on the side of criminals, criticizing its public security policy.

item.title: Governo Lula e estados negociam subsídio extra de R$ 1,20 por litro de diesel importado
item.description: The Lula government is negotiating with states an additional subsidy of R$ 1,20 per liter of imported diesel, to be split equally between the federal government and states, as an alternative to reducing ICMS. The measure, to be discussed at Confaz on March 27, would add to existing benefits (PIS/Cofins zero and R$ 0.32/liter subsidy), totaling R$ 1.84/liter in fuel price relief. The estimated impact is R$ 3 billion over two months.

item.title: Moraes concede prisão domiciliar a Bolsonaro após parecer da PGR e apelo de Michelle
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted humanitarian house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro, who is hospitalized in Brasília due to health complications. The decision followed a favorable opinion from the Prosecutor General's Office and an appeal by former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro. Additional highlights include the trial that could render former Rio governor Cláudio Castro ineligible and the expected extension of the INSS CPMI, to be reviewed by the Court.

item.title: Tornozeleira, visitas restritas e sem celular e redes; veja regras da domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted house arrest to former President Jair Bolsonaro for humanitarian reasons, but imposed strict conditions: electronic ankle monitor, ban on cell phones and social media, and restricted visits limited to family and lawyers. The ruling aims to uphold public order while addressing Bolsonaro’s health needs.

item.title: Justiça mantém condenação de criminalista que chamou Moraes de ‘advogado do PCC’
item.description: São Paulo's 3rd Private Law Chamber upheld the defamation conviction of criminal lawyer Celso Machado Vendramini for calling Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes a 'PCC lawyer'. The statements, made during a 2023 jury session, were ruled as exceeding legitimate criticism and carrying personal, ideological, and defamatory intent. The court affirmed the R$ 50,000 moral damages award, dismissing Vendramini's claim of professional immunity.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro chama prisão domiciliar temporária de ‘exótica’ e Carlos vê ‘migalha ditatorial’
item.description: Flávio and Carlos Bolsonaro criticized the STF's decision to grant temporary house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro, calling it 'exotic' and a 'dictatorial crumb,' respectively, despite expressing relief that their father can recover at home. Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos-SP) welcomed the move, emphasizing the importance of humane, family-centered care for the former president's recovery.

item.title: Lula sanciona Lei Antifacção e ressalta prisão de magnatas do crime
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the Anti-Gang Law, which increases penalties for participation in criminal organizations, facilitates asset seizure, and removes benefits such as amnesty and voting rights for gang leaders, even without final conviction. Lula emphasized that the law targets 'crime magnates' operating from luxury apartments, not just poor youth, and praised the Federal Police’s investigative expertise. He also noted discussions with U.S. President Donald Trump on transnational crime cooperation.

item.title: Governo arrecadará R$ 4,4 bi com tributos de fintechs, bets e JCP
item.description: The Lula government projects to collect R$ 4.4 billion in 2026 from taxes on fintechs, sports betting (bets), and interest on own capital (JCP), as announced by Federal Revenue Secretary Robinson Barreirinhas. Measures include raising the CSLL for financial institutions and increasing the JCP rate from 15% to 17.5%, plus taxation of GGR from bets. Despite this, the government estimates a primary surplus of only R$ 3.5 billion in 2026, far below the budget target of R$ 34.3 billion, due to tax benefit cuts and freezing of discretionary spending.

item.title: Lula sanciona PL Antifacção com apenas 2 vetos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the Anti-Gang Bill with only two vetoes: one to prevent the criminalization of individuals not linked to criminal organizations and another regarding the unconstitutional allocation of Funad funds. National Public Security Secretary Chico Lucas stated the vetoes uphold constitutional integrity and emphasize targeting organized crime leaders, including 'drug lords' and financiers, with implementation measures to be developed within 180 days.

item.title: PM morta em SP: Tarcísio fala pela 1ª vez sobre o caso e defende prisão do tenente-coronel
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) spoke for the first time about the killing of soldier Gisele Alves Santana by her husband, Lieutenant Colonel Geraldo Leite Rosa Neto. He defended the arrest of the military officer and condemned the crime, emphasizing the need for strict justice against domestic violence, particularly within the security forces.

item.title: Flávio questiona prisão domiciliar temporária de Bolsonaro: “Decisão exótica”
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) called the STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes' decision to grant 90 days of humanitarian house arrest to former President Jair Bolsonaro for broncopneumonia recovery 'exotic'. While acknowledging it as a first step toward justice, Flávio criticized the measure as inadequate. The remarks were made in an interview with GloboNews.

item.title: Malafaia poupa Moraes ao comentar prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Pastor Silas Malafaia, known for frequent criticism of Justice Alexandre de Moraes, adopted a conciliatory tone when commenting on the decision to allow Jair Bolsonaro to serve his sentence under house arrest. Rather than attacking the magistrate, Malafaia limited his remarks to the benefit granted to the former president, refraining from extending criticism to the Supreme Court.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula participa de 3ª Conferência Nacional de Desenvolvimento Rural
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva attends the 3rd National Conference on Sustainable and Solidary Rural Development in Brasília, alongside Minister Paulo Teixeira and other officials. The event, held from March 24–27, aims to develop policy proposals for rural, water, and forest populations, following over 40,000 participants in preparatory stages and the generation of around 1,000 public policy proposals.

item.title: Lula sanciona o PL Antifacção com vetos sobre a divisão de recursos e equiparação de penas
item.description: President Lula signed the Anti-Faction Bill but vetoed provisions that would have equalized sentences between members and non-members of criminal factions and directed seized assets to state and federal district funds, citing risks of criminalizing social movements and reducing federal revenue. The core of the bill, which toughens penalties and creates new crimes against criminal organizations, remains intact. The vetoes may be overridden by Congress in a joint session.

item.title: Com a saída de Ratinho, se Zema caminhar ao centro poderá surpreender
item.description: With the potential withdrawal of Ratinho Junior from the presidential race, Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (NOVO) could surprise by positioning himself in the ideological center, appealing to the 'neither-nor' electorate. Although Felipe Nunes of Quaest describes a 'calcified' electorate favoring Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, about one-third of voters remain uncommitted, opening space for a moderate alternative.

item.title: Lula escala Messias para se aproximar de Mendonça e ajudar a construir pontes no STF
item.description: President Lula plans to nominate Attorney General Jorge Messias to the STF to ease tensions in the Court, particularly with Justice André Mendonça, with whom Messias shares evangelical affiliation and a history of conflict with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. The move comes amid a dispute over the extension of the INSS CPI, linked to the Banco Master scandal, which will be voted on by the STF plenary next Thursday. Lula aims to use Messias’ nomination as a bridge to calm the polarized atmosphere in the Supreme Court, after delaying the nomination due to political resistance.

item.title: Justiça decide se Flávio Bolsonaro terá de pagar R$ 15 mil por uso indevido de foto com crianças
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Justice will decide this Wednesday (25) whether to uphold the ruling that ordered Senator Flávio Bolsonaro to pay R$ 15,000 in moral damages to photographer Ana Carolina Fernandes for the unauthorized use of a 2018 photo of two children. Bolsonaro shared the image in 2020 with a caption claiming the girls were 'militants against faith' and had their minds 'seized by cultural Marxism,' distorting the original anti-Jair Bolsonaro protest context. Although he deleted the post after backlash, the first-instance court already ruled he violated copyright and harmed the photographer’s honor.

item.title: Após conversa com Caiado, Kassab se reunirá com Leite na 4ª feira
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, said he will meet with Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite on Wednesday (March 25, 2026) to discuss a potential presidential candidacy for Leite in 2026. The meeting comes a day after Kassab spoke with Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado, also mentioned as a possible PSD presidential candidate. The activity follows the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior as a pre-candidate due to family reasons.

item.title: MDB é o partido da democracia e tem projeto, diz Baleia Rossi
item.description: MDB president Baleia Rossi stated the party is 'the party of democracy' and has a project for Brazil during a solemn session celebrating its 60th anniversary. He praised historical figures like Tancredo Neves, José Sarney, Ulysses Guimarães, and Michel Temer for their courage in building Brazilian democracy. Founded in 1966 during the dictatorship, the MDB was the only permitted opposition party and played a central role in redemocratization, though it will not field its own presidential candidate in 2026.

item.title: PT quer atacar imagem de um Flávio “moderado”
item.description: The PT's executive committee has decided to attack the image of moderation surrounding Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president in 2026, aiming to dismantle the perception that he is distinct from his father, Jair Bolsonaro. The strategy was defined in a meeting on Tuesday (24) and targets voters who see Flávio as a more moderate alternative to Bolsonarism.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei antifacção sem vetos polêmicos, como proibição de voto e de auxílio-reclusão
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the anti-gang crime law without vetoing controversial provisions, including the ban on voting and prison assistance for pretrial detainees. The measure aims to strengthen the fight against organized crime, retaining disputed articles that sparked debate in Congress.

item.title: Cassado pela Câmara, Deltan Dallagnol anuncia pré-candidatura ao Senado no Paraná
item.description: Deltan Dallagnol (Novo) announced his pre-candidacy for the Senate in Paraná, following the annulment of his 2023 mandate by the TSE, though he remains eligible. His candidacy is part of a PL-led 'Lava Jato' slate, which includes federal deputy Felipe Barros as Senate candidate and former judge Sergio Moro as pre-candidate for governor, backed by PL president Valdemar Costa Neto and attended by Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que endurece combate a facções criminosas com dois vetos
item.description: President Lula signed the Anti-Faction law, which toughens penalties against organized crime by defining criminal factions as groups of three or more using violence or intimidation to control territories, increasing sentences to up to 40 years in prison. Lula vetoed two provisions: one allowing individuals to be charged without proof of faction ties (due to concerns over criminalizing social movements), and another directing seized assets to state and federal district funds, arguing it would undermine the National Public Security Fund.

item.title: Tarcísio celebra prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro e defende “tratamento humano”
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas praised Justice Alexandre de Moraes' decision to grant Jair Bolsonaro 90 days of humanitarian house arrest due to bronchopneumonia, stating it is fair to ensure the former president receives proper medical treatment.
